findings.description: Section s1 contains code examples using `ApplicationException`, `HttpRequest`, and the `@OnError` annotation, but none of these appear in the hints array. `ApplicationException` is thrown when the file list is empty, `HttpRequest` is the object from which `getPart()` is called, and `@OnError` appears on the DB-registration action method. Users searching for any of these identifiers would not be routed to s1.
findings.source_evidence: Source: `throw new ApplicationException(...)` in the server-side retrieval example; `public HttpResponse doMoveFile(HttpRequest req, ...)` in the same section; `@OnError(type = ApplicationException.class, path = "forward://RW11AC0601")` in the DB-registration example.

findings.description: Section s1 consolidates five distinct h2-level source sections (JSP implementation, server-side retrieval, file saving, input-stream reading, and DB-registration overview) into a single section. The automated pre-check also flags 'Section count 5 < source headings 6'. While no content appears to be lost, s1 is exceptionally large and covers multiple independent topics, which may reduce retrieval precision when a user asks about only one of those sub-topics (e.g., input-stream reading vs. file saving).
findings.source_evidence: Source h2 (===) headings: ファイルアップロード画面の作成方法（JSP）, アップロードファイルの取得方法（サーバサイド）, アップロードファイルの保存方法, アップロードファイルを読み込む方法, アップロードファイルをDBに登録する方法 — all mapped to s1.
